name: nodejs
title: nodejs
summary: evented I/O for V8 javascript - runtime executable
description:
evented I/O for V8 javascript - runtime executable
Node.js is a platform built on Chrome's JavaScript runtime for easily
building fast, scalable network applications. Node.js uses an
event-driven, non-blocking I/O model that makes it lightweight and
efficient, perfect for data-intensive real-time applications that run
across distributed devices.
.
Node.js is bundled with several useful libraries to handle server
tasks:
.
System, Events, Standard I/O, Modules, Timers, Child Processes, POSIX,
HTTP, Multipart Parsing, TCP, DNS, Assert, Path, URL, Query Strings.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node because evented I/O for V8 javascript - runtime executable.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `node [options] [v8-options] [-e string | script.js | -] [--] [arguments ...]` as documented upstream for node.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `node inspect [-e string | script.js | - | <host>:<port>] ...` as documented upstream for node.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `node [--v8-options]` as documented upstream for node.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install nodejs. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `node [options] [v8-options] [-e string | script.js | -] [--] [arguments ...]`. Further invocations from upstream: `node inspect [-e string | script.js | - | <host>:<port>] ...`. evented I/O for V8 javascript - runtime executable.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: evented I/O for V8 javascript - runtime executable.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: evented I/O for V8 javascript - runtime executable.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install nodejs
Install with: sudo apt install nodejs
